title: MacOS M3 - Invalid Symlink when building image
body:
### Issue Description

When building an image that contains a symlink, I get invalid symlink error.

```
podman build -f ./Containerfile --network=host -t symlink-test .
Error: invalid symlink "/var/tmp/libpod_builder1345862155/build/docs" -> "../docs"
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create the following directory structure to reproduce:
```
app/
├── Containerfile
└── docs -> ../docs/

docs/
└── docs.txt
```
where the Containerfile is simply:
```
FROM scratch
COPY . .
```
and the symlink was created with `ln -s ../docs` from the `app` directory.

2. Within the `app` directory, run:
``` podman build ./Containerfile . ```



### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Image should build with the appropriate directories and files copied.

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: The symlink must stay within the build context. The build is not allowed to access files outside of the context AFAIK thus the error.
This is a security feature to prevent symlink attacks, especially when using podman-remote.
